steps.title: Find the misrepresentation
steps.content: The sentence argues that merely counting yearly patents gives a false impression of innovation, because truly novel patents are vastly outnumbered by nonnovel ones. The blank must express that distortion.

steps.title: Reject unrelated verbs
steps.content: Choice B means predicts a future event. Choice C means prevents or blocks. Choice D means slanders or speaks badly of. The verb meaning contradicts or gives a false impression of fits the patent-count argument. The answer is A.
